description: DutchTrainer curriculum structure from A0 to B1. This file is a planning map and does not replace current app content.

levels.title: First Dutch Survival
grammarGoals: Recognize Dutch sounds and common spelling patterns
Use basic pronouns: ik, jij, hij, zij, wij
Understand simple word order in short statements
Use zijn and hebben in memorized phrases
Recognize de and het as noun markers
Ask and answer very short yes/no questions

lessonGroups.name: greetings
lessonGroups.goal: Say hello, goodbye, please, thank you, and introduce yourself.

lessonGroups.name: numbers
lessonGroups.goal: Use numbers 0-20 and recognize simple time words.

lessonGroups.name: family and food
lessonGroups.goal: Name close family members and order very simple food or drinks.

lessonGroups.name: basic places
lessonGroups.goal: Recognize common places and ask where something is.

levels.title: Everyday Basics
grammarGoals: Use present tense regular verbs
Use common irregular verbs: zijn, hebben, gaan, kunnen, willen
Build simple main clauses with subject-verb order
Ask wh-questions with wie, wat, waar, wanneer, hoe
Use negation with niet and geen
Use basic plural forms
Use possessives: mijn, jouw, zijn, haar, onze
Use simple prepositions of place

lessonGroups.name: family and daily routine
lessonGroups.goal: Talk about people, routines, and simple daily activities.

lessonGroups.name: food and shopping
lessonGroups.goal: Buy basic items, ask prices, and describe simple preferences.

lessonGroups.name: transport and city
lessonGroups.goal: Use simple travel phrases and ask for directions.

lessonGroups.name: housing and appointments
lessonGroups.goal: Describe a home, make simple plans, and confirm times.

lessonGroups.name: health and weather
lessonGroups.goal: Describe basic feelings, simple symptoms, and weather.

levels.title: Everyday Independence
grammarGoals: Use separable verbs in common contexts
Use perfect tense for common past events
Use modal verbs with infinitives
Use want, omdat, maar, and en to connect ideas
Use comparative and superlative adjectives
Use object pronouns in simple sentences
Use time expressions for recent past and near future
Understand common word order changes after conjunctions

lessonGroups.name: work and study
lessonGroups.goal: Talk about jobs, school, schedules, and simple responsibilities.

lessonGroups.name: health and services
lessonGroups.goal: Explain simple problems and handle everyday service situations.

lessonGroups.name: past events
lessonGroups.goal: Describe what happened using common perfect tense patterns.

lessonGroups.name: future plans
lessonGroups.goal: Make plans, talk about intentions, and arrange times.

lessonGroups.name: opinions
lessonGroups.goal: Give simple opinions and reasons about familiar topics.

lessonGroups.name: travel and housing
lessonGroups.goal: Handle travel, rental, and neighborhood conversations.

exampleLessonNames: I Think It Is Too Expensive

levels.title: Confident Everyday Communication
grammarGoals: Use subordinate clauses with correct verb placement
Use past tense and perfect tense in familiar narratives
Use future constructions with gaan and zullen
Use relative clauses with die and dat
Use reflexive verbs in daily contexts
Use common passive-like constructions and impersonal phrases
Use opinion structures with reasons and examples
Understand common idiomatic phrases in everyday speech

lessonGroups.name: work and education
lessonGroups.goal: Discuss experience, goals, problems, and preferences at work or school.

lessonGroups.name: health and appointments
lessonGroups.goal: Explain symptoms, follow advice, and manage appointments in detail.

lessonGroups.name: opinions and reasons
lessonGroups.goal: Express opinions clearly and support them with reasons and examples.

lessonGroups.name: past events and future plans
lessonGroups.goal: Tell short stories and discuss future plans with context.

lessonGroups.name: news, society, and culture
lessonGroups.goal: Understand and discuss simple news, cultural habits, and public topics.

lessonGroups.name: problem solving
lessonGroups.goal: Describe issues, negotiate solutions, and explain what needs to happen.
